mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and stimulates credit creation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following below-median FEDFUNDS levels are not positive in greater than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and a stable or declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate healthy demand without labor market stress, jointly amplifying corporate revenue confidence and producing positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month increase paired with UNRATE flat or falling
failure_condition: The joint signal's 20-day return hit rate is not significantly greater than the best single-proxy hit rate (paired t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery regimes, consecutive monthly increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal rebounding manufacturing utilization and corporate earnings inflection, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery periods, positive 60-day returns following 2-month INDPRO increases occur in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend monthly retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer spending momentum that translates into near-term revenue surprises for consumer-facing firms, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following above-median RSAFS growth are not positive in greater than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% signals contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns after the trigger are not negative in greater than 50% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 1% generates outsized positive equity returns because accommodative monetary policy amplifies real earnings growth via low discount rates; the joint effect dominates either signal in isolation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increasing over 2 months while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: The joint signal's 20-day return hit rate does not exceed the product of the two single-signal hit rates by at least 10 percentage points over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2% annualized indicates aggregate demand is outstripping sluggish supply, lifting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth above 2% are not positive in greater than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.
